comments.body: Any idea why I'm getting this error?

I changed the  openSession()  method call to  getCurrentSession()  but I'm facing a different issue now. The collections in my entity are lazily inititialized. So when I try to access any of the lazily initialized fields of the entity I get "org.hibernate.SessionException: Session is closed!" . Is there any way to avoid this problem?
Where are you closing the session?  Are you moving to a different thread?

No I don't close the session at all. But it throws this exception "org.hibernate.SessionException: Session is closed!". This happens only when I use " sessionFactory.getCurrentSession() ". But with " sessionFactory.openSession() " it works in the right way.

That is because the ThreadLocalSessionContext closes the session when you commit the transaction.  If you want to use that particular implementation of current_session_context_class then you will need to have one transaction for the lifetime of the session you are working with.

answers.body:
Calling openSession() does not associate the resulting Session object with Hibernate's current session context. However, if you call getCurrentSession() and no session exists, then Hibernate will open a new session and associate it with the context.

In other words, you need to replace your call to openSession() with a call to getCurrentSession().

title: "org.hibernate.HibernateException: Illegal attempt to associate a collection with two open sessions" even when the context is Thread
body:
In my application(server) I create a Hibernate session in the beginning of request processing using "sessionFactory.openSession()". I retrieve the object make some changes to it in some other class and then do a saveOrUpdate as follows:

Session session = sessionFactory.getCurrentSession();
Transaction dbTransaction = session.getTransaction();
dbTransaction.begin();
session.saveOrUpdate(entity);
dbTransaction.commit()

I have also set the below property in the configuration file. 

<property name="hibernate.current_session_context_class">thread</property>

However when I do a session.saveOrUpdate(entity) I get the below exception. I think the reason for the below exception is "getCurrentSession()" is not getting the session which I opened using "sessionFactory.openSession()" but I am not able to understand why it is not getting the currentSession which I opened using "sessionFactory.openSession()" when the request processing begins in the server.
